question_text:
Two different forces are applied to two surfaces with different areas:
- Force A: 1000 N on an area of 10 m²
- Force B: 800 N on an area of 4 m²

Which force produces higher pressure?
correct_answer: Force B produces higher pressure. Force A: 100 Pa, Force B: 200 Pa. Force B is twice as high.
explanation: Calculate pressure for each force separately using P = F ÷ A. Force A = 1000 ÷ 10 = 100 Pa. Force B = 800 ÷ 4 = 200 Pa. Even though Force B is smaller, it acts on a much smaller area, creating higher pressure.
